business.headline: AI reality check hits Asia as Seoul's Kospi crashes 8%, Japan's Nikkei tumbles 5%South Korea's KOSPI fell 8.2%, prompting authorities to halt program trading for 20 minutes after steep losses rippled across the market. Japan's Nikkei 225 dropped 5%, while Hong Kong's Hang Seng declined 2.4%.

What's the one big hurdle delaying India-US trade deal? Piyush Goyal explainsPiyush Goyal said India will bring the US trade deal into force only after Washington creates a tariff framework that favours Indian exporters over regional competitors. The position follows US legal changes that overturned reciprocal tariffs and forced both sides to revisit implementation.

Sebi may allow celebrity endorsements for financial brands. But here's a catchUnder the proposal, celebrities would be allowed to endorse a financial firm's brand or company name, subject to prescribed conditions, disclaimers and prior approvals.

Stock market holiday on Muharram: Are NSE and BSE open for trading on June 26?Planning to trade on Friday, June 26? With Muharram being observed across the country, many investors are wondering whether the NSE and BSE will remain open for trading?

Don't want to pay taxes online? Here's how to use the 'Pay at Bank Counter' optionNot comfortable paying taxes online? You're not alone. The Income Tax Department still allows taxpayers to pay their dues at authorised bank branches through the 'Pay at Bank Counter' facility.

India-US trade deal very close, but here's why it isn't signed yetPiyush Goyal said India and the US are close to finalising their trade deal. India wants tariff terms that give its exporters a clear edge over rivals.

Dalal Street pares gains: Sensex ends flat after climbing over 600 pointsEarly optimism driven by lower crude oil prices and a stronger rupee faded as investors booked profits, though benchmark indices still managed to end in positive territory.

Zerodha's Nithin Kamath has a warning for you against pyramid schemesAccording to figures cited by Kamath, more than 5.5 crore Indians have lost their savings to over 5,300 such schemes. The estimated losses stood at around Rs 10 lakh crore as of 2015, and he suggested that the actual figure could be much higher now.

Gold loses over Rs 51,000 from all-time high. Why is the yellow metal crashing?Gold has fallen sharply from its peak as silver also comes under selling pressure. The correction is being driven by rate-hike bets, a stronger dollar and shifting investor appetite.

United Spirits may cut 100 jobs as Diageo looks to trim costsUnited Spirits is set to lay off around 100 employees as Diageo pursues a global cost-cutting programme. The move comes amid a wider restructuring drive in India despite strong growth and continued investment in premium brands.

Amazon to invest $48 billion in India by 2030, CEO Andy Jassy meets PM ModiAccording to Jassy, the company aims to support 3.8 million jobs in India by 2030 and help generate $80 billion in cumulative e-commerce exports.

Sensex jumps over 600 points: Why are stock markets rallying today?Benchmark indices extended their recovery on Thursday as softer crude, firm Asian cues and gains in banks and autos lifted sentiment. The move has eased near-term macro concerns, though investors remain watchful of the monsoon and global central bank signals.

EPFO portal shutdown from June 26-28: How it will affect your EPF claimsPlanning to withdraw money from your EPF account or submit a claim this week? You may have to postpone it. EPFO has announced a three-day shutdown of key portal services as it upgrades its claims processing system.

GTA 5 made $10 billion. Here's what GTA 6 could earn if it repeats historyGrand Theft Auto V is the second best-selling video game ever made. As pre-orders open for its successor, here is what the numbers say about how big GTA 6 could really be.

7 reasons why you must file ITR even if your income falls below exemption limitThink your income is too low to require an ITR? Not necessarily. Certain financial transactions can make return filing mandatory even if your income is below the exemption limit.

Explained: Why this investor calls India's stock market a poison marketAfter years of making money in the stock market, ace investor Shankar Sharma has delivered one of his strongest warnings yet. Here's why he believes Dalal Street is becoming a poison market.

Oil at pre-Iran war lows lifts Dalal Street; Sensex and Nifty jumpThe S&P BSE Sensex climbed 447.36 points, or 0.58%, to 77,438.58, while the NSE Nifty50 advanced 136.90 points, or 0.57%, to 24,158.55 as of 9:28 am.

RBI says banks must notify pensioners before recovering excess pensionThe RBI has told banks to notify government pensioners before recovering any excess pension paid into their accounts. The amendment seeks to stop sudden debits and set a clearer recovery process through notice and consent.

Lost money to online fraud? RBI's new rule might just save youThe RBI has set new rules on who bears losses from fraudulent electronic banking transactions. Customers who report quickly can get full reversals or capped compensation under a time-bound process.

Stock mismatch, mega offshore deals: What ED found during raids on Rajesh ExportsThe Enforcement Directorate searched nine premises linked to Rajesh Exports in Bengaluru and Mumbai under FEMA. The agency says missing records, stock gaps, trade set-offs and suspicious trades are now under investigation.

entertainment.headline: Man found dead at Bigg Boss Kannada contestant Krishi Thapanda's Bengaluru home A 45-year-old man, identified as Vaishak Upadhyaya, was found dead by suicide at Bigg Boss Kannada Season 5 contestant Krishi Thapanda's Bengaluru home while she was away attending a meditation programme.

Chauhaan teaser X reaction: Ajay Devgn sparks meme fest, comparisons to Dhurandhar Actor Ajay Devgn's Chauhaan first look has sparked mixed reactions on X, with many users trolling the teaser. The response has split viewers between propaganda criticism and support for his return to action.

While it's an interesting idea, the film gets pulled down by randomness.

Baahubali 3 loading? Prabhas, Rana Daggubati, Anushka hint at big sequel news A viral clip from Baahubali: The Torchbearer, the franchise documentary, has sparked speculation about expansion of the Baahubali universe. In the clip, Rana Daggubati teases more from the franchise, while Prabhas is seen flashing three fingers, leaving fans wondering if it hints at Baahubali 3.

Singer Chinmayi praises husband Rahul's gender post with powerful marriage note Singer Chinmayi Sripada backed her husband, actor-director Rahul Ravindran, after he defended the themes explored in The Girlfriend and spoke about women's everyday struggles. She said women should find a man like him instead of settling and suffering.

Chiranjeevi, Nagarjuna, Trisha at Khushbu Sundar's daughter's wedding; Vijay skips Several stars, including Chiranjeevi, Nagarjuna Akkineni, Trisha Krishnan among others, attended Khushbu Sundar's daughter's wedding in Goa. Take a look at the videos here.

Ila Arun backs Alka Yagnik after viral wheelchair video: Art lives in the soul Singer Ila Arun has defended singer Alka Yagnik after comments doubted her ability to sing following a viral wheelchair video and the singer's health update. Her message underlined support for Yagnik as she celebrated her Padma Bhushan and spoke about battling sensorineural hearing loss.

SRK grooves to Jhoome Jo Pathaan, DDLJ tracks at Mangalore event. Viral videos Shah Rukh Khan performed some of his tracks at a special event in Mangalore. Several videos from the event have emerged online.

Dharman: Rajinikanth begins shooting for Ashwath Marimuthu film, new poster out The makers of Dharman unveiled a new Rajinikanth poster as shooting began on Thursday. The new update regarding the film to build anticipation among the fans.

Sense and Sensibility trailer: Daisy Edgar-Jones leads new Jane Austen adaptation The first trailer introduces Daisy Edgar-Jones as Elinor Dashwood alongside the family's changing fortunes and key suitors. It sets up the sisters' bond, romantic tensions and a looming clash between Colonel Brandon and Willoughby.

He disclosed how he aimed to create a prison unlike anything audiences had seen before, while giving the reality show a distinct visual identity.

Are Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce getting married on July 4? Here's the truth A fresh report has linked permits, hotel bookings and venue preparations to Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's rumoured New York wedding. The reported July 3 and July 4 arrangements have intensified speculation, though the couple remain publicly silent.

Welcome To The Jungle box office prediction: Can Akshay score another comedy hit? Akshay Kumar returns to the Welcome franchise with Welcome To The Jungle as its advance booking opens on a cautious note. The film's box-office prospects now rest on nostalgia, spot bookings and audience word of mouth.

Dhanush congratulates Mammootty on Padma Bhushan with a cake-cutting celebration Actor Dhanush and the Om team congratulated actor Mammootty on the film's set after his Padma Bhushan honour. Photos shared by Dhanush's production company captured the celebration, including a handshake and cake-cutting.

In the Hand of Dante review: Oscar Isaac, Gal Gadot's ambitious drama loses its gripRelease Date:Wed Jun 24, 2026 For cinephiles drawn to literary adaptations and bold swings, director Julian Schnabel's In the Hand of Dante offers striking visuals and committed performances worth sampling. Does the film do justice to the noble but uneven journey through hell, purgatory and fleeting paradise? Here is our review.

Rare photos of OTT stars before they became household names Before they became OTT sensations, Jitendra Kumar, Mithila Palkar, Sumeet Vyas, Shriya Pilgaonkar and Amol Parashar had remarkably different lives. Take a look at their rare throwback photos and inspiring journeys to fame.

But despite seeking swift justice, he said the family still could not believe the allegations and had noticed no warning signs before the wedding.

Denmark plans azaan ban. It fears turning into a 'suburb of Islamabad'Denmark has revived plans for a nationwide ban on the Islamic call to prayer, coming months after the European country outlawed the Islamic full-face veil in public spaces. The proposal comes amid concerns over growing Islamisation in Denmark.

Hitler killed Jews; Jews used Hitler's weapons to defeat ArabsOne of history's greatest ironies unfolded in 1948. Weapons originally designed for Hitler's Nazi Germany, which killed nearly 6 million Jews, ended up helping defend the newly formed Jewish state of Israel. Jews sourced Nazi-era weapons and planes from Czechoslovakia, and beat back the armies of five Arab states.

Are BJP governments allergic to eggs? Here's the reality with evidenceThe BJP government in West Bengal plans to involve ISKCON in preparing Kolkata school midday meals, which might see eggs move out of the menu. Some other BJP states earlier removed eggs from midday meals. Is the BJP allergic to eggs? Here's a detailed analysis with facts.

Mine began with passport verificationAfter the MEA said passports are only travel documents, I was reminded of my police verification that turned into an interrogation over my nationality. In a video message, he accused one Kunal Singhaniya of taking money from him and a few others making false job promises.

Officials said the case shifted from an accidental death to suspected murder after inconsistencies, CCTV footage and digital evidence surfaced.

Security guard tells Gurugram CEO his salary, gets honest career advice in returnAkash Gupta, founder and CEO of Zypp Electric, shared a video on social media with the text overlay: "Founder poaching his own employee," showing him speaking to a security guard employed at his office in Gurugram.

Senior IPS officer Mahesh Dixit named new Intelligence Bureau chiefSenior IPS officer Mahesh Dixit has been appointed as the new Director of the Intelligence Bureau, bringing decades of experience in intelligence operations, counter-terrorism and internal security.

Shhh! The secret careers inside luxury brands that no one talks aboutFashion and luxury industries offer several unique careers beyond designers and models. 8 arrested hours after FIR filed in alleged Ram temple donation theftThe Uttar Pradesh Police on Thursday arrested all eight accused in the alleged Ram Temple donation embezzlement case, hours after registering the first FIR based on the preliminary findings of a Special Investigation Team (SIT).

Venezuela quake toll rises to 235 as rescuers race to find survivors under rubble UpdatesThe Venezuelan government said at least 235 people have died, more than 4,300 have been injured and around 200 remain trapped after twin earthquakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 struck about 160 km west of Caracas.

sports.headline: Barely past the group stage, FIFA World Cup 2026 rewrites goalscoring records The FIFA World Cup 2026 has already entered the record books. Barely past the group stage, the expanded tournament became the highest-scoring edition in history. Lionel Messi leads the goalscoring charts at the moment with 5 strikes to his name.

Australia advance after Paraguay draw as USA top Group D despite Turkey defeat FIFA World Cup: Australia booked their place in the Round of 32 after holding Paraguay to a hard-fought 0-0 draw in their final Group D match on Thursday, while the United States finished top of the group despite suffering a dramatic 3-2 defeat to Turkey in Los Angeles.

Anushka Yadav's heavy metal miracle at The Kalinga At the Kalinga Stadium, 18-year-old Anushka Yadav broke the women's hammer throw national record twice in one night, throwing 67.02m to qualify for the 2026 Asian Games. She announced herself as one of India's brightest field-event prospects.

Man City agree club-record deal with Forest to sign World Cup star Elliot Anderson Manchester City have agreed a club-record deal worth £116 million with Nottingham Forest to sign England midfielder Elliot Anderson, with the 23-year-old set to undergo a medical before completing his move to the Etihad Stadium.

National holiday declared in Ecuador after country qualifies for WC knockouts Ecuador declared a national holiday after their dramatic 2-1 comeback win over Germany which sealed them a place in the FIFA World Cup knockout rounds for the first time in 20 years. President Daniel Noboa hailed the team's resilience in a difficult campaign.

Netherlands top Group F as Japan and Sweden reach knockouts The Netherlands sealed top spot in Group F with a 3-1 win over Tunisia, while Japan booked their place in the last 32 after a 1-1 draw with Sweden. Sweden also progressed as one of the best third-placed teams, setting up knockout ties for both the Dutch and Japan.

All eyes on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi as Shreyas Iyer makes his T20I captaincy debut India begin a new T20I era under captain Shreyas Iyer against Ireland in Belfast, with attention split between leadership transition and 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's potential debut. Ireland, led by Lorcan Tucker, also eye opportunities for young players as both teams deal with transitions and challenging playing conditions.

Australia reach knockouts, USA top Group D despite loss to Turkey Australia (AUS) vs Paraguay (PAR) highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: USA and Australia qualify for the Round of 32 of the FIFA World Cup. Australia held Paraguay to a 0-0 draw, while the USA lost to Turkey 2-3 in a last-gasp goal by Kaan Ayhan. Despite the loss, USA top Group D.

Iran seek knockout berth as coach Amir Ghalenoei avoids Pride celebration debate Iran aim to make their nation proud against Egypt with a place in the World Cup knockout stages on the line, coach Amir Ghalenoei said on Thursday, while refusing to be drawn into the debate over the Pride celebrations surrounding the match.

FIFA World Cup 2026 shatters attendance record despite US travel mess The 2026 FIFA World Cup has become the most-attended edition in history, crossing 3.6 million spectators with nearly half the tournament still to play. The milestone arrives despite criticism over expensive tickets, visa hurdles and logistical concerns across the United States.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India debut faces rain threat in Belfast? Here's the weather forecast IND vs IRE, 1st T20I: Rain could threaten India's first T20I against Ireland in Belfast, with weather forecasts predicting intermittent showers. The comeback secured Sebastián Beccacece's side a place in the knockout stages as one of the tournament's best third-placed teams.

Nicolas Pepe's brace closes curtain on Curacao, creates history for Ivory CoastNicolas Pepe struck twice as Ivory Coast beat Curacao 2-0 to reach the FIFA World Cup knockout stage for the first time. Curacao bowed out after an inspiring debut campaign despite a spirited display.

FIFA World Cup 2026: Seattle's Pride Match puts Egypt vs Iran clash in spotlightFIFA said fans can carry rainbow flags into the Egypt-Iran World Cup match in Seattle. The decision has sharpened attention on Pride celebrations opposed by both teams' federations.

Animesh Kujur leads India's Asiad qualification charge on Day 2Animesh Kujur and several others met the AFI's Asian Games standards on Day 2 of the Inter-State Championships. Sindhushree G's national record in the pole vault underlined the growing competition for India's Aichi-Nagoya squad.

Mohammad Amir starts afresh as British citizen, joins T20 Blast as domestic playerMohammad Amir has begun a new phase of his career after joining Notts Outlaws in the T20 Blast as a domestic player following the acquisition of British citizenship, allowing him to play county cricket without occupying an overseas slot.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to get India debut in Belfast T20I? Batting coach breaks silenceIndia batting coach Sitanshu Kotak said Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will get his chance when the time is right, insisting the team will not drop an in-form player just to hand the 15-year-old his debut.

ECU 2-1 GER Highlights: Ecuador upset Germany, Ivory Coast advanceEcuador (ECU) vs Germany (GER) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (ECU 2-1 GER) Ecuador refused to let their World Cup dream die, overturning an early deficit to stun Germany and sneak into the knockout stages as one of the best third-placed teams. In the other Group E clash, Ivory Coast also made history by reaching the last 32 for the first time.

The claim keeps an absurd subplot alive, but England's real issue remains finishing chances on the pitch.

Keeping his case for the number 3 spot alive? Sai Sudharsan smashes 100 against SL ASai Sudharsan hit 132 for India A against Sri Lanka A in the ongoing unofficial Test series. The innings revived his Test No. 3 case and justified management's patience amid criticism.

Trouble for India? Australia may get huge boost for high-voltage Lord's clashWomen's T20 World Cup: Australia head coach Shelley Nitschke said that Phoebe Litchfield may return against India on June 28 at the Lord's, saying that that left-handed batter has recovered well from her calf injury.

Asian Games silver medallist Yarraji targets gold after comeback from ACL injuryJyothi Yarraji marked her return from an ACL tear with a 12.99-second hurdles win at the National Inter-State Championships. Why Jannik Sinner wore a cooling vest at training ahead of WimbledonJannik Sinner used a cooling vest during Wimbledon preparations due to the London heatwave, which has also impacted tournament operations, causing a Hawk-Eye outage and drawing criticism from players including Eva Lys.
